reviews.author: Dr.Hemendra Agrawal- Knee &Shoulder Doc- ACL expert
reviews.text: Vinpearl Safari Phu Quoc is one of the finest wildlife parks in Southeast Asia and an absolute must-visit when in Phu Quoc. Spread over a vast, beautifully maintained area, the safari offers a rare opportunity to see animals in open, natural-style habitats rather than cramped enclosures. The highlight is the open safari tram, where visitors ride through large zones while giraffes, zebras, deer, rhinos, and other animals move freely around the vehicle. It feels safe, well-managed, and incredibly exciting—especially for children. The guides are knowledgeable and share interesting facts, making the ride both educational and enjoyable. The walking zoo area is equally impressive, with clear pathways, shaded rest areas, and excellent cleanliness throughout. Animal enclosures are spacious, well designed, and clearly labeled. The park strongly focuses on conservation, animal welfare, and breeding programs, which is evident in how healthy and active the animals look. Facilities are top-class, including clean restrooms, food courts, souvenir shops, and stroller-friendly paths. Staff members are polite, helpful, and always ready to assist, adding to the overall positive experience. The park is well organized, with enough seating and refreshment options to relax between zones. Overall, Vinpearl Safari Phu Quoc delivers a world-class wildlife experience that combines entertainment, education, and conservation. It is perfect for families, nature lovers, and anyone seeking a memorable and meaningful day in Phu Quoc. Highly recommended! 🌿🦒

reviews.author: Beyzanur Erdoğan
reviews.text: We bought our tickets from one of the ticket booths along the road. We had some issues trying to purchase them online. On the way to the safari area, there are physical ticket sellers — you can easily buy your tickets there. I honestly had a lot of fun at the safari. There are bird shows and dance performances at specific times during the day, so make sure to check the schedule. However, I must mention that some of the cats’ cages were seriously small, which is a major issue in my opinion. After entering with your ticket, you need to queue near the bus area. From there, you board the safari buses that take you inside the enclosed areas for the safari experience. But DEFINITELY bring cash with you. You don’t need to buy an extra ticket to feed some of the animals — you can purchase food inside and feed them. The only problem is that the food can only be bought with cash. Other than that, it was a very enjoyable experience. The close interaction with the animals is especially impressive. I highly recommend attending the bird show — don’t miss it.

reviews.author: senay atak
reviews.text: Zoos often end up being disappointing in one way or another, but this place is not like that at all. You can walk through it inside a forest, and it’s very nicely planned. It’s small enough not to make you tired, yet big enough not to feel boring — an ideal size. The variety of animals is very good, and seeing how healthy they looked really put our minds at ease. I especially loved the giraffes — they have a large area and you can feed them. We arrived early and there was a small crowd at the entrance, but getting around by train was very comfortable. Everything is well-organized and well-planned. When we left around 1 PM, there was no line at the ticket counter anymore. Of course, visiting in the morning or late afternoon might be better because of the heat, and the animals are more active at those times. I definitely recommend this place. It was fun and we still talk about the moments about the animals. We bought the tickets from counter and there was a que. And my husband waited for the entrance que and waited for 15 minutes. Therefore it would be better to have tickets online.

reviews.author: JIMMY CHOI
reviews.text: Despite the rain ruining parts of the day, our VIP tour turned out to be truly unforgettable — thanks entirely to our exceptional guide. We were really looking forward to the VIP experience, but heavy rain led to the cancellation of the bird show, and the combination of wet weather, crowded facilities, and long waits made much of the visit feel frustrating and unpleasant. However, our dedicated VIP tour guide completely turned the day around and made it one of the best zoo experiences we’ve ever had. She was highly knowledgeable, spoke Korean and English fluently with perfect ease, and her warm, patient, professional, and genuinely kind attitude made all the difference. She went far beyond expectations to ensure we still enjoyed a rich, meaningful, and fun time despite the weather setbacks — sharing fascinating facts and treating us with such care that we felt truly special. We even took a photo together, and although I unfortunately don’t remember her name, her outstanding service is what will stay with us forever. The company should be extremely proud of her — she truly deserves praise and our deepest thanks. Without her, the day would have been far less enjoyable (or even quite disappointing). We highly recommend the VIP tour, especially if you’re lucky enough to have her as your guide! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

reviews.author: Born_Surgeon
reviews.text: Vin safari is a kind of zoo. It has two parts. One you can visit by walking or can take tuk tuk ride. I would advise to go by walking so that you can explore the zoo at your own pace. Try to reach by 9 o'clock in the morning if u r doing both vin safari and vin wonder on the same day. Once you finish zoo by walking, you will reach at vin safari by bus. It's a restricted area where you will take a ride by bus. Try to finish it by 12-1 pm and reach vin wonder. At win wonder, there are 5-6 sections. Important ones are the sea shell (must watch mermaid show) , adventure World and typhoon world. Try to finish 1st two by 3 pm and go to typhoon world (water park). Water park will close by 5.30 pm. My personal take: day 1. Vin safari and grand world Day-2. Vin wonder If u r travelling through agent pre booking, make sure your agent doesn't put all three in one day. It will be exhausting. Mine was same. So I shifted grand world to other day.

reviews.author: Jaroslav PTAAH
reviews.text: Safari Phu Quoc is one of those places where you go in thinking “this will be nice” and leave thinking “okay, that was actually really fun” 😄 Beautiful surroundings, lots of attractions, and animals casually living their best lives while you drive around like a very curious guest. It’s well organized, spacious, and there’s always something interesting happening — from feeding times to unexpected “wow, that’s closer than I thought” moments. Great for families, animal lovers, and anyone who enjoys a mix of nature, fun, and light adventure without breaking a sweat. Verdict: A pretty place with plenty to see, lots to do, and more smiles than expected. Definitely worth a visit! 🦒🌿

reviews.author: Rajeev Jha
reviews.text: This is a must-do in Phu Quoc! I booked a combo ticket (entry, zoo tram, and F&B) via Klook. The staff were incredibly friendly and helpful, often going out of their way to assist. While not everyone speaks fluent English, using Google Translate or knowing a few Vietnamese phrases makes things easier. During the animal bus tour, I was asked to leave a quick review and received a coupon to feed the elephants — such a fun experience! The zoo was very clean and enjoyable to explore. The food at the Giraffe Restaurant was average, but the giraffe views made up for it. Free shuttle buses run frequently, so definitely take advantage of them 🙂

reviews.author: Manisha Suru
reviews.text: Vinpearl Safari Phu Quoc was one of the highlights of our trip. The safari bus tour was impressive — seeing animals like lions, rhinos, and giraffes roaming freely while we were safely inside the bus felt exciting and well organized. But the absolute best part was the giraffe feeding experience. Getting so close to them, feeding them by hand, and watching how gentle they are was unforgettable. It’s something both adults and kids will genuinely enjoy. Totally worth the extra cost. The park is clean, spacious, and well maintained. We spent around 3–4 hours comfortably without feeling rushed. It’s not cheap, but considering the overall experience and the scale of the park, it felt worth it. Go early in the morning to avoid heat and crowds. Highly recommend if you’re visiting Phu Quoc

reviews.author: ahmad shukri che mamat
reviews.text: Vinpearl Safari Phú Quốc is truly a unique experience in Vietnam's open zoo and safari park. Walking through the grounds, you’ll be amazed by the variety of animals roaming freely in natural habitats. From majestic tigers and powerful lions to playful bears, graceful deer, striking zebras, and even massive rhinoceroses, the safari offers a rare chance to see wildlife up close in a way that feels both exciting and educational. The park is well-designed to showcase biodiversity and conservation, making it a great destination for families, animal lovers, and anyone seeking a memorable adventure. The animals appear healthy and active, and the safari atmosphere gives you the impression of stepping into a miniature Serengeti right in Phú Quốc. One aspect that could be better is the tram service. While the tram is essential for exploring the safari zones, the waiting time can be quite long, especially during peak hours. Many visitors end up waiting in line for extended periods, which can dampen the overall experience. Improving the frequency and efficiency of tram operations would make the visit smoother and more enjoyable. From my personal point of view, Vinpearl Safari Phú Quốc is a must-visit attraction on the island, offering unforgettable encounters with wildlife. With some improvements to the tram service, it could easily become one of the most seamless and world-class safari experiences in Asia.

reviews.author: Yd Yx
reviews.text: Come if you love to see wild animal. The area is quite big so walking can be exhausting especially under the sun. The safari bus is thrilling seeing wild animal in semi natural environment. You can touch some of the friendly animal as well. Worth visiting

reviews.author: Matija R.
reviews.text: VinPearl Safari is a fun activity to do while in Phu Quoc. It takes a little while to get to from the city, but it is worth it. The park is split up into the regular zoo area, with a few animals you can feed/interact with, and the semi-wild safari. You can immediately notice the attention to detail and the care that is provided to the animals. It looks like they have a lot of space and activities in their pens that suit them specifically. They look taken care of and have freedom to roam around and socialize with other animals of their kind. You can feed the lemurs, giraffes and elephants, with lemurs being the most exciting in my opinion. The issue with the park is overcrowding. Too many people are allowed each day, and not everyone is respectful to the animals. Additionally, both the giraffe and elephants food ran out when we visited since they sold it all out before noon. The price of a regular ticket is also pretty steep for Vietnam standards, but if you compare it to some other country it isn’t as bad. The semi-wild safari is also an amazing experience. These animals are kept in even larger areas and are left to truly roam around. Even their eating habits are also replicated as best they can. Some animals also share large plots of land, like zebras, rhinos, antelopes etc. Overall, the safari is a great option for a day trip, better than regular amusement parks that are also available on the island.

reviews.author: Keri Caldwell
reviews.text: The zoo was average, what you would expect from any zoo. The safari was unique and a fun experience. The bus is air-conditioned and you are able to get out of your seat to view and take photos of the animals if you wish. The bus drives through each separate enclosure and will drive slower/faster to help you get a good view of the animals. We were able to see giraffes, zebras, tigers, bears, lions, rhinos, and more from the window on the bus. The safari lasts about 20-30 minutes.

reviews.author: Nhi Lai
reviews.text: Run and get the ticket!!! This is definitely a must-do in Phu Quoc! I booked the combo ticket (entrance + zoo tram + F&B) through Klook. Let’s start with the staff- everyone was amazing and super friendly! They were so helpful and really went above and beyond. Some staff don’t speak much English, so learning a few Vietnamese phrases or using Google Translate will definitely make your visit smoother. When we took the bus to see the lions, zebras, and other animals, one of the staff asked me to do a quick web review (I think it’s only in Vietnamese, not 100% sure), and I got a food coupon for the elephants in return. We had so much fun feeding them! The place was super clean and enjoyable! We had such a great time exploring all the different areas of the zoo. However, the food at the Giraffe Restaurant was a bit meh, it was dry and not served hot, so I wasn’t a big fan. But honestly, the amazing view of the giraffes kind of made up for it! There are multiple buses to get you to the zoo and it is for free so TAKE ADVANTAGE OF IT :)

reviews.author: Karthikeyan L
reviews.text: A moderate sized park with diverse wildlife exhibits. There is a bird show in the morning at 10:00 am and another one at 2:00 pm. I would recommend for you to reach 15 - 20 mins earlier at the amphitheater and settle at a good spot for the show. If you want to experience the show and record it find a spot at the center or if you want to get up-close with some hornbills the fly in from left and right top corner stairs of the amphitheater. The show showcased some amazing birds like rhino hornbill, great hornbill and ostriches. After the show there are lots of animal enclosures to explore. If you are with elderly people or infants you can opt the buggy service for 1,00,000 VND. But the buggy doesn't stop at the enclosures instead the just drive buy the enclosures so you cannot get a up-close encounter with the animals. At the end of the park there is a bus safari which goes through different gates which holds tigers, lions, bears, rhino, giraffes, zebra and many other herbivores. But the bus as well doesn't stop next to the animals for you to observe theme properly. They just drive by each gates without giving you a proper chance to observe the animals especially if there is a long queue at the bus station. Yes of course you have to wait in a long queue for your turn at the bus station unless you have a express pass. There is also a bird aviary and a giraffe restaurant where you can feed the giraffes. Overall a good place for kids and sub adults to enjoy up-close with various animals.

reviews.author: Tatjana Lukso
reviews.text: I liked the zoo, especially the giraffes and lemurs; they were hand-fed. Seeing the other animals in cages, I didn't feel very comfortable. I'd like to see better conditions for them, maybe a larger area. But it's 100% true that all the animals are well-fed and looked after.

reviews.author: Shivani Jain
reviews.text: This was the best experience in phu quoc if you have a baby.. our baby loves animals so it was a worthy visit. Animals are kept with good care. Better to visit early so you can see animals in active mode. If you buy vip access do take the service on the counter because it so confusing and no one tells anything neither anything is written anywhere. We had to do 3 rounds of the park because we got so confused. Anyways it was one of the best experience.

reviews.author: Ravi Ladia
reviews.text: Vinpearl Safari Phú Quốc is an outstanding attraction and easily one of the highlights of the island. The diversity of animal species is impressive, with well-maintained habitats that allow you to see a wide range of wildlife in a setting that feels open and thoughtfully designed. What truly sets this safari apart is the closeness with which the animals can be seen. Whether on the safari ride or while walking through the park, the viewing experience is immersive and engaging, making it memorable for both adults and children. The safari experience itself is very well executed, offering a unique opportunity to observe animals up close in a calm, organized, and educational environment. If you plan to purchase a VIP pass, it is more efficient to buy it online in advance to save time and avoid queues. That said, it is highly recommended to walk through the park and explore all areas, as this allows you to fully appreciate the layout, exhibits, and overall atmosphere at your own pace. Overall, this is an amazing experience for visitors of all ages—well worth the time and money, and a must-visit when in Phú Quốc.

reviews.author: Tatyana Radygina
reviews.text: I felt like a happy child after visiting the safari zoo! One part of the park has spacious open enclosures for most animals where some of them can be fed with extra food you buy on site (unfortunately, it can run out). The main safari zone is fully dedicated to the animals, you explore it on a special bus. Buses run regularly, but there can be a 15-20 minute wait in a queue; there’s shade, fans, and water available nearby. Plan about 30 minutes for the bus tour.

reviews.author: Afiqah Sakinah
reviews.text: We fell in love with this place, the zoo area are kept clean, facilities well maintained, plants are trimmed and all animals are well fed. We can tell that they are happy, total time spent here for us is 4 hours. We managed to circled the area and went on the safari ride. Our daughter loves this place. We fed the giraffe at the giraffe restaurant and the elephants. Will definitely com again ❤️

reviews.author: Michi
reviews.text: Unfortunately, the overall experience was very disappointing. We visited both the safari and zoo, and many of the animals appeared malnourished and lethargic. The grounds were dirty and poorly maintained, which was upsetting to see. The layout is also not stroller- or wheelchair-friendly, with many stairs and no clear signage indicating alternative routes, making navigation difficult. After the safari tour, we went to the giraffe restaurant intending to feed the giraffes after lunch. However, by the time we finished eating at around 1pm, the giraffe food had already sold out. Leaving many of us waiting in long queues only to be turned away with anger. The toilets are wet and super dirty. The important signage and the animal show is in vietnamase. Overall, I would not recommend this place. Given the condition of the animals, facilities, it felt like a waste of money.

reviews.author: Naomi Fuller
reviews.text: Fantastic place. The animals are very well cared for. There's a zoo part too and the enclosures and aviary are amazing. On the bus going around the safari park really feels like a safari!!!! Highly recommend

reviews.author: Tejas Lone
reviews.text: Vinpearl Safari Phu Quoc is a remarkable destination that combines wildlife conservation with an unforgettable visitor experience. Located on the northwest coast of Phu Quoc Island, Vinpearl Safari is Vietnam’s largest semi-wildlife conservation park, home to over 4,000 animals across 200 species and more than 400 plant varieties. The park is designed to give animals spacious, naturalistic habitats, allowing visitors to observe them in conditions that closely resemble the wild. One of the highlights is the open safari tour, where guests ride through vast enclosures and encounter giraffes, zebras, rhinos, and lions roaming freely. This immersive experience feels far more authentic than a traditional zoo. Families especially enjoy the interactive zones, where children can feed friendly animals like elephants and deer under staff supervision. The park also hosts engaging animal shows and educational programs that emphasize conservation and respect for nature. The facilities are well-maintained, with shaded rest areas, restaurants, and convenient transport options inside the park. Visitors often praise the staff’s professionalism and the park’s commitment to animal welfare. Overall, Vinpearl Safari Phu Quoc is not just an attraction—it’s a meaningful journey into the world of wildlife. It offers both entertainment and education, making it a must-visit for nature lovers and families alike.

reviews.author: Christian Wa
reviews.text: The park itself is quite beautiful and designed in a relatively natural way. For an Asian zoo, many of the enclosures are fairly spacious, with sufficient shade, and the animals are kept at a reasonable distance from visitors. This makes the visit feel a bit less uncomfortable compared to many other zoos in the region. That said, some enclosures still appear too small, and you do notice typical stress-related behaviors of captive animals; for example, bears pacing in small circles for long periods of time. As expected, the park is clearly designed for mass tourism. It is perfectly possible to get around without walking much at all, as buggies transport visitors everywhere, which many tourists happily use. This unfortunately also attracts a certain type of visitor: people trying to touch or feed wild monkeys despite clear rules against it, people cutting in line, and people feeding elephants or giraffes ice cream or sweets. In one case, even a staff member was unable to stop a woman from feeding her purchased snacks to a giraffe. On the positive side, food and drink prices inside the park are very reasonable and not overpriced. The staff genuinely tries to prevent inappropriate behavior and rule violations, although they are not always successful due to the sheer rudeness and disregard of some visitors. Overall, a visually appealing safari with decent infrastructure, but the experience is somewhat affected by mass tourism and irresponsible visitor behavior.

reviews.author: Lisa Phuong
reviews.text: The safari was really fun as they have lions, rhinos, giraffes and more. If you go atva certain time, you can buy food to feed the giraffe and elephant. The park has many animals from a variety of monkeys, birds, tiger, hyena, lions and more. Some of the enclosures, I thought was small. Mostly pay in cash at all the food and drink stand. There is a fun bird show that's on twice a day. We came here twice. Once we brought tickets at the door which there was a short queue and the second time, we had a combo ticket with vinwonders with unlimited access for 3 days at Vinwonders and safari.

reviews.author: Y K
reviews.text: Very good zoo. Very large territory. Consists of two parts, Open Zoo and Safari Park. Visiting the Safari Park is only possible in closed buses, which travel through various closed zones where animals live in a natural environment in a large territory. Although the territory is large and all the predators were sleeping, they were still all visible from the bus window. On the bus, you can also hear information about the visible animals in 2-3 different languages (there are many language packages and the languages presented are chosen according to which nationalities dominate the bus at that moment). It probably takes several hours to walk through the entire Open Zoo area. Small open buses also travel in the Open Zoo area (for an additional fee), but they generally do not stop in front of the cages and you cannot examine the animals and birds for a longer time. The cafe where you can feed the giraffes is very popular. We went to the morning bird show and it was very good. I recommend going right in the morning when it opens, because then there are a little fewer people and it is not so hot yet. The parking lot is quite small considering the number of visitors, we left at 1:00 PM and then there were no more spaces for cars in the parking lot, even for scooters there were few spaces. The website is a bit confusing and I couldn't find a page with specific information about the entire zoo - a plan of the zoo, where and when something is happening. There is a bird show twice a day and as I understand it there are also animal feedings, but I couldn't find any information about where and when exactly. There are also probably special evening visits, but the information about that is also very chaotic. Everywhere there are websites focused only on selling tickets for that, and some ticket sales agents' websites have better information than the zoo's own website. There are numerous different ticket packages offered online, there are no such ticket combo packages at the box office on site. You buy an entrance ticket at the box office and you buy all other additional services inside the zoo according to your wishes and needs. I have a feeling that you won't get any special discounts if you buy tickets in advance online.

reviews.author: SUMEET VERMA
reviews.text: Wow what a womderful place it is. Simply breathtaking. Right from the entry gates you get the feeling that its gonna be something surreal. The place is enormous boosting everything from reptiles to birds to mammals. The main attractions are the bird sanctuary and the girraffe stand next to a restaurant where you can feed the girraffes but you have to buy their treats ofcourse. There is a buggy service which can be opted with a fee. And for a more personal and insightful experience i would suggest you buy the VIP tickets in advance. You get your own private buggy and a personal guide who takes you around the place. One drawback that i personally feel should be looked upon is that the leopard and tiger cages seem too small for them.Even the area designated for birds and reptiles seem too small almost feels cruel sometimes. Overall great experience. Fun for evreryone kinda activity. Tip: do remeber to be there in the evening as the place holds a stunnig african dance performance.

reviews.author: raja singam
reviews.text: ✨ A Magical Half-Day at Vinpearl Safari Phu Quoc ✨ From the moment you arrive, the experience feels smooth and premium 🌿. The entrance is impressive, and the best part—no long waiting time 🙌. You’re allowed to carry snacks and water, which is super convenient, especially if you’re visiting with kids 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦. 👉 Pro tip: Apply online in advance—it saves time and effort. --- 🚗 VIP Safari Ride – Totally Worth It Once inside, it’s highly recommended to upgrade to the VIP pass 🚘. You’ll be taken around in a special safari car, with discounted pricing for children under 1.2 meters. The car covers all major zones No stops in between, but 👀 you can see animals and birds clearly A relaxed, guided experience without walking fatigue --- 🦎 Touch, Feel & Thrill Moments The safari isn’t just about watching—it’s interactive and exciting: Reptile zone 🐊: You can touch the iguana 🦎 and enjoy the crocodile watch from a bridge— looking down at massive crocodiles in the water below is pure adrenaline! 😲 Elephant meet & greet 🐘: One of the most heart-warming moments 💛 The elephants are friendly—you can touch and interact with them. --- 🦒 Giraffe Encounter at the Graffiti Hotel Next stop is the Graffiti Hotel area, where magic continues ✨ You can touch and feed giraffes 🦒—an unforgettable experience, especially for kids 📸😍. --- 🐯 The Wild Safari – The Ultimate Highlight The most exciting part is the Wild Safari, which is free with your ticket 🎟️. Travel in an AC bus 🚌 A short queue, but totally worth the wait You’ll see tigers, lions, and bears up close 😮🐅🦁 A true “humans in cage, animals free” experience—thrilling and safe! --- 🐦 Bird Show Timings Don’t miss the bird show 🎭🦜 11:00 – 11:30 AM 2:00 – 2:30 PM It’s fun, informative, and perfect for all ages. --- 🌟 Final Thoughts ⏱️ It takes around half a day to explore everything comfortably. The park is well maintained, clean, and thoughtfully designed. Kids absolutely love the animals, and adults enjoy the calm + thrill balance 🌈. If you’re visiting Phu Quoc, this safari is 100% worth it— a mix of adventure, learning, and unforgettable memories 🐾💚✨

reviews.author: ste ashby
reviews.text: Not the worse zoo/safari I’ve been to by any stretch of the imagination. They announce themselves as the biggest Zoo on an island. Many of the enclosures are quite small, or have insufficient animals inside for them to be suitably stimulated. That said, I always keep an eye on how the animals move or pace around their environment and there wasn’t many signs of distress from the animals. The bird show was pleasant and the food from Flamingo restaurant was reasonable with good portion size. Worth a visit if you need a day away from the beaches and swimming pools.

reviews.author: Іра Сливанюк
reviews.text: Vinpearl Safari Phú Quốc is an amazing experience and one of the highlights of the island! The park is huge, green, and very well maintained. The staff are friendly and the organization is excellent. A special thanks to the safari bus guide, who was very friendly, knowledgeable, and engaging, sharing interesting facts and making the ride even more enjoyable. It’s a perfect place for families with children as well as adults — educational, beautiful, and truly atmospheric. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Hiep Nguyen
reviews.text: The best safari or zoo I've been go compared to all the ones in North America. So many animals that I grew up watching on TV and Netflix you get to see up close. We had a group of 11 so last only 2.5hrs before the young kids wanted to go back to hotel 😅. We didn't get to do the last part of the safari, the line up was 100+ ppl long infront of us and another 30ppl behind us. It was enjoyable over all. It could definitely be more efficient with the ticketing and how staff are trained to explain things. 4.5 stars over all.
